SEARCH
Package com.dua3.utility.fx.controls

Class InputControlState<R>

java.lang.Object
com.dua3.utility.fx.controls.InputControlState<R>
Type Parameters:
R - the type of the value being managed
Direct Known Subclasses:
ListInputControlState, ObjectInputControlState
public abstract class InputControlState<R> extends Object
The InputControlState interface encapsulates the value, validation logic, error message, and validity state of an InputControl.

  • Property Summary

    Properties
    Type
    Property
    Description
    protected final javafx.beans.property.StringProperty
    error
    Provides access to the property that holds error messages related to validation.
    protected final javafx.beans.property.BooleanProperty
    required
    Provides a property representing whether the input is required or not.
    protected final javafx.beans.property.BooleanProperty
    valid
    Returns the property indicating the validity of the input control's state.
    protected abstract javafx.beans.property.Property<@Nullable R>
    value
    Provides access to the property representing the current value of the input control.

  • Property Details

    • required

      protected final javafx.beans.property.BooleanProperty requiredProperty
      Provides a property representing whether the input is required or not. The property's value determines if the input control mandates a value.
      See Also:
      • isRequired()
      • requiredProperty()

    • value

      protected abstract javafx.beans.property.Property<@Nullable R> valueProperty
      Provides access to the property representing the current value of the input control. The value can be updated or observed for changes.
      See Also:
      • getValue()
      • setValue(R)
      • valueProperty()

    • valid

      protected final javafx.beans.property.BooleanProperty validProperty
      Returns the property indicating the validity of the input control's state.
      See Also:
      • isValid()
      • validProperty()

    • error

      protected final javafx.beans.property.StringProperty errorProperty
      Provides access to the property that holds error messages related to validation. This property can be used to observe or update the error state of an input control.
      See Also:
      • getError()
      • setError(String)
      • errorProperty()

  • Constructor Details

    • InputControlState

      protected InputControlState(Supplier<? extends @Nullable R> dflt, Function<? super @Nullable R,Optional<String>> validate)
      Constructs an InputControlState instance with a provided default value supplier and a validation function to validate the input's value.
      Parameters:
      dflt - A Supplier that provides the default value for the control's state. This can supply null if no default value is needed.
      validate - A Function that applies validation logic to the input value. It accepts a value of type R or null and returns an Optional containing an error message if validation fails, or an empty Optional if the value is valid.

  • Method Details

    • requiredProperty

      protected final javafx.beans.property.BooleanProperty requiredProperty()
      Provides a property representing whether the input is required or not. The property's value determines if the input control mandates a value.
      Returns:
      a BooleanProperty that indicates whether the input is required
      See Also:
      • isRequired()

    • valueProperty

      protected abstract javafx.beans.property.Property<@Nullable R> valueProperty()
      Provides access to the property representing the current value of the input control. The value can be updated or observed for changes.
      Returns:
      the property holding the value of type R, which may be null
      See Also:
      • getValue()
      • setValue(R)

    • validProperty

      protected final javafx.beans.property.BooleanProperty validProperty()
      Returns the property indicating the validity of the input control's state.
      Returns:
      a BooleanProperty representing whether the current state is valid.
      See Also:
      • isValid()

    • errorProperty

      protected final javafx.beans.property.StringProperty errorProperty()
      Provides access to the property that holds error messages related to validation. This property can be used to observe or update the error state of an input control.
      Returns:
      a StringProperty representing the current error message. If no error is present, the property will hold an empty string.
      See Also:
      • getError()
      • setError(String)

    • validateFunction

      protected final Function<? super @Nullable R,Optional<String>> validateFunction()
      Returns a function that performs validation on the value managed by this InputControlState. The function accepts a value of type R or null and returns an Optional<String> representing the validation result. If the Optional is empty, the value is considered valid; otherwise, it contains an error message describing the validation failure.
      Returns:
      a function used to validate the value of this control state

    • validationListeners

      protected final Collection<Runnable> validationListeners()
      Returns a collection of validation listeners associated with the input control state. These listeners are executed whenever the validation logic is triggered, allowing for custom behaviors or updates in response to validation changes.
      Returns:
      a collection of Runnable instances representing the validation listeners

    • defaultValueSupplier

      protected final Supplier<? extends @Nullable R> defaultValueSupplier()
      Provides a supplier for the default value of the control's state.
      Returns:
      a Supplier that supplies the default value, or null if there is no default value.

    • invalidateState

      public void invalidateState()
      Invalidates the current state of the input control and triggers a validation of its value. This method ensures that the validation logic is executed on the JavaFX application thread.

      The validation process may update the control's validation status, error messages, and invoke any associated validation listeners. By default, this operation is scheduled to run later on the JavaFX thread, ensuring thread safety.

      It uses the PlatformHelper.runLater(Runnable) utility to schedule the validation logic.

    • addValidationListener

      public void addValidationListener(Runnable listener)
      Adds a validation listener to the collection of listeners associated with the input control state. The provided listener will be executed whenever the validation logic is triggered, allowing for custom behaviors or updates in response to validation changes.
      Parameters:
      listener - a Runnable instance representing the validation listener to be added

    • removeValidationListener

      public boolean removeValidationListener(Runnable listener)
      Removes a validation listener from the collection of validation listeners associated with the input control state. If the specified listener is present, it will be removed.
      Parameters:
      listener - the Runnable instance representing the validation listener to remove
      Returns:
      true if the listener was removed successfully, false otherwise

    • reset

      public final void reset()
      Resets the state of the input control to its default value and triggers validation.

      This method invokes the default value supplier to retrieve the default value and assigns it to the input control's state through the setValue method. After setting the default value, it performs validation by calling the validate method to ensure the state reflects the appropriate validation results.

    • validate

      public boolean validate()
      Validates the current value of the input control using the provided validation function. Updates the validity state and error message based on the result of the validation. Executes any registered validation listeners after the validation process.
      Returns:
      true if the value is valid; false otherwise

    • isValid

      public final boolean isValid()
      Checks if the current state of the input control is valid. This method evaluates the validity based on the value of the validProperty.
      Returns:
      true if the input control's state is valid, otherwise false

    • getValue

      public @Nullable R getValue()
      Retrieves the current value of the input control managed by this state. The returned value represents the current state of the control's input and can be null.
      Returns:
      the current value of type R, or null if no value is set

    • isRequired

      public final boolean isRequired()
      Determines whether the input control mandates a value. This method checks the value of the required property to indicate if input is required.
      Returns:
      true if the input is required, otherwise false

    • setValue

      public void setValue(@Nullable R arg)
      Sets a new value for the input control's state. Updates the current value managed by the valueProperty.
      Parameters:
      arg - the new value of type R to set, or null if no value is provided

    • isEmpty

      public boolean isEmpty()
      Determines whether the input control's value is considered to be empty.
      Returns:
      true if the input control's value is empty, otherwise false.

    • setError

      protected void setError(String s)
      Updates the error property with the provided error message and adjusts the validity state based on whether the error message is empty or not.
      Parameters:
      s - the error message to set; an empty string indicates no error

    • getError

      public final String getError()
      Retrieves the current error message associated with the input control's state. The error message typically represents the result of the validation process.
      Returns:
      the current error message as a String, or an empty string if no error is present

    • voidState

      public static InputControlState<Void> voidState()
      Creates and returns an InputControlState instance specifically designed for inputs that have no associated value (i.e., Void type). This state model is initialized with a default value supplier that always provides null.
      Returns:
      an InputControlState<Void> instance representing an input control state that doesn't necessitate a specific value.
